title: Alcea loftusii
taxonomy: Alcea loftusii is classified under the family Malvaceae. This classification places it within the order Malvales and the clade Rosids. The genus Alcea is often referred to as the hollyhock genus due to its similar characteristics to the common hollyhock. As a member of the Malvaceae family, Alcea loftusii shares its classification with other plants that produce similar flowers and fruits. The classification of Alcea loftusii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.

intro: Alcea loftusii, also known as Perennial Hollyhock, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alcea loftusii include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as saponins and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Alcea loftusii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat conditions like eczema, acne, and irritable bowel syndrome.
